// Negative fixture for spec 120 R6 scan (h), rejection-path matcher
// (round 11, O3): a bare `id` identifier (and a bare `.ID` selector)
// rendered raw INSIDE the idvalidate rejection branch must be flagged
// even though the suffix matcher (specid/beadid/epicid) misses it; the
// idrender-wrapped sibling stays green.
package fixture

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/mrmaxsteel/mindspec/internal/idvalidate"
	"github.com/mrmaxsteel/mindspec/internal/idvalidate/idrender"
)

func rejectRawly(id string) error {
	if err := idvalidate.BeadID(id); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("invalid bead id %s: %w", id, err) // bare id — RED
	}
	return nil
}

type beadRec struct{ ID string }

func rejectSelectorRawly(b beadRec) error {
	err := idvalidate.BeadID(b.ID)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("invalid bead id %s: %w", b.ID, err) // bare .ID — RED
	}
	return nil
}

func rejectSafely(id string) error {
	if err := idvalidate.BeadID(id); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("invalid bead id %s: %w", idrender.Bead(id), err) // GREEN
	}
	return nil
}
